items.headline: City releases youth services master plan and library facilities update
items.summary: San José presented status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan and Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9th. These reports outline the city's priorities for youth programming and library services going forward.

items.headline: Major zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: Mountain View's Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 was cancelled, pushing back a development project at 333 Franklin Street. The project will be rescheduled for a future date to be announced.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant agreement
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7th. This infrastructure project addresses traffic and rail safety at a key intersection.

items.headline: City Council advances Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring
items.summary: Cupertino is developing a new Active Transportation Plan to improve biking and walking infrastructure. The council discussed Phase 2 updates and revised scoring criteria that will determine which projects get prioritized and funded.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract for unspecified project
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution to approve project plans and award a construction contract, though the agenda summary does not specify which project or its scope. Residents should monitor follow-up reporting to learn what infrastructure or development is being built.

items.headline: Milpitas considers major update to federal housing and development plan
items.summary: The council reviewed a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 budget, which governs how federal housing and community development funds are spent in Milpitas. This could affect affordable housing priorities and grant funding for local projects.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a major $8.8M senior housing development with 65 units at 678 E St John St, addressing San José's growing need for affordable senior living options. This is one of the largest new residential projects in the recent permit batch.

items.headline: City Council adopts new emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: San José City Council approved an Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy on April 1st. This policy determines where the city will place temporary housing solutions, which could affect multiple neighborhoods.
